prompt: Deserialization throws InvalidClassException. What is the bug?
choices: Config must implement Externalizable instead of Serializable
Without an explicit serialVersionUID, adding/changing fields causes the JVM to generate a different UID, making previously serialized data incompatible
Serializable classes cannot have int fields
The class must be public to be serialized
explanation: Java computes serialVersionUID automatically based on the class structure. When the class changes (add/remove fields, change types), the computed UID changes and existing serialized data becomes incompatible. Fix: always declare private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; explicitly. You can then manage version compatibility yourself.
